paper_title: Every planar graph with $Δ\geqslant 8$ is totally $(Δ+2)$-choosable
summary: The conjecture that every simple graph $G$ satisfies $\chi''_\ell(G) \leq \Delta(G) + 2$ is the list-coloring analogue of the Total Coloring Conjecture and remains open in general. The source paper itself proves it for planar graphs with $\Delta \geq 8$ (Corollary 1), extending Borodin's 1989 result for $\Delta \geq 9$. No follow-up paper resolving the full general conjecture was found in web searches conducted up to May 2026.
search_queries: list total chromatic number conjecture chi_l'' Delta+2 graph theory
arxiv 1904.12060 citing papers list total coloring planar graph
notes: The List Total Coloring Conjecture is a major open problem analogous to the long-standing Total Coloring Conjecture (Behzad–Vizing). The source paper's main result (planar graphs, Δ ≥ 8) is one of the strongest known partial results. Web searches found no paper proving or disproving the full statement; the conjecture is old enough (3+ years) that medium rather than high confidence is assigned.
